channel.title: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD)
channel.description: Daily Feed (Last 48 Hours, Updated 9 AM BD) generated by script

item.title: How good is infant formula?
item.description: Perhaps less than its marketers say

item.title: The ‘Tibet Aid’ Cadres System as a Mechanism for Political Control in Tibet
item.description: The system, supposedly meant to aid Tibet, actually serves to sideline local Tibetan personnel while boosting the careers of Han Chinese cadres from other regions.

item.title: Why Bangladesh Is Fencing Its Border With Myanmar
item.description: A non-state actor – the Arakan Army – now controls Myanmar’s side of the border. That forces Bangladesh to rely more on unilateral forms of border management.

item.title: How (Not) to Conserve Tropical Forests
item.description: Although the Tropical Forest Forever Facility that emerged from the last United Nations Climate Conference is unlikely to succeed, critically important global conservation efforts are not doomed. Through sustainability-linked sovereign bonds and loans, tropical countries can overcome the TFFF’s fatal flaws.

item.title: What Will the End of the Iran-US War Mean for Pakistan?
item.description: Pakistanis are hoping that their role in providing Trump with an offramp to end the war will lead to investment flows.

item.title: Beijing’s Bullying of Taiwan Threatens Ocean Ecology
item.description: China managed to secure Taiwan’s exclusion from a Track 2 platform designed to address maritime ecology. It’s a worrying escalation of China’s political warfare.

item.title: North Korea’s Quiet Campaign to Be a ‘Responsible’ Nuclear Power
item.description: As U.N. sanctions enforcement fragments, Pyongyang is waging a second contest – over status – that is visible only in the original Korean.

item.title: How Saudi Arabia Derailed Pakistan’s Africa Drive
item.description: Pakistan’s aspirations in Africa are growing, but its ability to realize them remains constrained by economic weakness, dependence on external financing, and the shifting priorities of its most important allies.

item.title: When the Davidson Window Meets the ‘Xi Window’
item.description: The Davidson Window measures China’s military capability to invade Taiwan. Arguably more important is Xi Jinping’s confidence in his military.

item.title: South Korea’s Nuclear Ambitions Clash With Political Realities
item.description: South Korea’s ruling party failed to secure key races, underlining the need to steer the country’s nuclear future with more than just political momentum.

item.title: In China, Christianity Is Treated as a Cult
item.description: The CCP has decided some fundamental tenets of Christianity mark out the world’s largest faith as a cult, and one that needs vigorous suppression.

item.title: Was Brexit Inevitable?
item.description: If counterfactual history is motivated by a refusal to accept what many have deemed inevitable, it is newly relevant now that the West is marking the tenth anniversary of the Brexit referendum. Even if Britain's fateful choice was caused by deeper structural and historical forces, that does not justify fatalism.

item.title: The Last Maoist: What Next for India?
item.description: A victory that leaves governance deficits intact, rehabilitation frameworks underperforming, extractive interests empowered, and surveillance tools normalized is not a full resolution.

item.title: The deceptive rise of the “short king”
item.description: Small men have won over social media, but continue to struggle in the dating market

item.title: From Space to the Polar Depths: China Aims for a Three-Dimensional Presence in the Arctic
item.description: The Arctic – where polar, deep sea, and space intersect – offers a particularly revealing case for China’s ambitions in the “strategic new frontiers.”

item.title: A Tale of Two AI Policy Regimes
item.description: Brazil wants regulation. Argentina rejects it.

item.title: Syria’s War Crimes Dilemma
item.description: The government can execute war criminals or get full international support—but not both.

item.title: Questioning the Just War Doctrine
item.description: With war becoming more frequent and more lethal to civilians, Pope Leo XIV and the College of Cardinals are wading into the debate over its legitimacy. The best outcome of the “consistory” Leo is convening in in late June would be to authorize an overhaul of the just war doctrine for the modern era—and ask the necessary questions.

item.title: Bosnia Must Stand on Its Own
item.description: The Trump administration's recent, apparently corrupt intervention in Bosnia and Herzegovina could have been an opportunity finally to put the country on a path to full independence after more than 30 years of being a ward of the international community. Instead, the vicious cycle of dependence looks set to continue.

item.title: Foreign Pressure, American Freedom
item.description: How other countries and their citizens helped the United States live up to its ideals.

item.title: Modi-Trump Meet Is Unlikely to Reset Relations Torn Over the Past Year
item.description: There are increasing questions over the reliability of the United States as a strategic partner.

item.title: The Strategic Logic of the AI Arms Race
item.description: AI and drones are rapidly transforming warfare, shifting military power toward countries that can combine manufacturing scale, advanced AI systems, and battlefield data. The United States and Europe, hobbled by political dysfunction and obsolete military structures, remain dangerously unprepared.

item.title: Orban’s Defeat Is Being Dangerously Misread
item.description: Panicking strongmen will turn to more extreme measures.

item.title: ASEAN and Russia Agree to Deepen Cooperation During Special Summit in Kazan
item.description: The global energy crisis has given a new momentum to relations between Moscow and the 11-nation Southeast Asian bloc.

item.title: The Quiet Rise of the Plastics Crisis
item.description: How to counter an insidious global threat.

item.title: When a Cease-Fire Is Really a Stalemate
item.description: Equilibrium with Iran is the best America can do.

item.title: Fighting with Ghosts: Sar Sokha’s Belated Sanctions Panic
item.description: Cambodia’s Interior Minister appears to have found an adversary in Washington: a sanctions list that no longer exists.

item.title: MSCI Raises New Transparency Concerns About Indonesia as Emerging Markets Verdict Looms
item.description: The global index provider has threatened to downgrade the country to "frontier market" status due to the lack of transparency in its stock market.

item.title: Islamabad’s Crisis of Control in Pakistan-Administered Kashmir
item.description: Grievances over political representation will remain a powerful means of mobilizing opposition against the Pakistani state.

item.title: Indonesia-Timor Leste Reconciliation: ‘Through Memory Towards Hope’
item.description: The fact that the two nations managed to reconcile after their painful history is a true feat of diplomacy – one that holds lessons for the world today.

item.title: Indian Joint Air Defense Doctrine: Implications for South Asian Stability
item.description: In the South Asian strategic environment, even defensive measures can generate negative outcomes.

item.title: Britain is not ready to rejoin the EU
item.description: After a lost decade, it is time to focus on the future

item.title: Anthropic’s astonishing commercial success makes it a target
item.description: The cultlike firm is battling with the government for control of superpowered AI
